Augmented.WrongSystem: Cyberpunk 2020: Augmented Edition requires the Cyberpunk 2020 system to be active.
Augmented.MissingApi: Cyberpunk 2020: Augmented Edition requires a newer Cyberpunk 2020 system that exposes the helper API (game.cyberpunk.api). Please update the base system.

CYBERPUNK.SaveNotOwned: You don't own {name} — only its owner or the GM can make this save.
CYBERPUNK.StabilizeNoGM: Stabilization succeeded, but no GM is connected to record it.
CYBERPUNK.StabilizeRelayed: Stabilization recorded — the GM will confirm it.
CYBERPUNK.ParryDeclareBody: The next incoming melee attack is automatically blocked. Parry is consumed on first use. The parrying character takes −3 to all other actions this turn. (CP2020 p.102)
CYBERPUNK.ActNowBody: Use the character sheet to fire weapons or take actions.
CYBERPUNK.SuppFireNoToken: Suppressive fire: the attacker's token isn't on the active scene, so the fire zone can't be placed. Drop the attacker's token on the canvas (or target tokens manually and use the evasion prompts).
CYBERPUNK.SuppFireZoneFail: Could not place the suppressive fire zone.
CYBERPUNK.FireZoneNotFound: Fire zone not found — it may have been removed.
CYBERPUNK.NoTokensOnScene: No tokens on the current scene.
CYBERPUNK.NoTokenTargeted: No token is targeted. Right-click a token → Target (or hover + T), then click Apply Damage again.
CYBERPUNK.TakeAimTitle: Take Aim (+1 to hit per consecutive round, max +3)
CYBERPUNK.TakeAimTitleActive: Aiming ({n}/3 rounds). Click to increment. Click at 3 to reset.
CYBERPUNK.WaitActTitle: Take delayed action — announce you are acting now
CYBERPUNK.WaitForTurnBtnTitle: Wait for Turn — skip this slot and act after a chosen combatant
CYBERPUNK.DodgeTitle: Declare Dodge (−2 to attacker's melee roll, costs your action)
CYBERPUNK.DodgeTitleActive: Dodging (−2 to attacker's melee roll this round) — click to cancel
CYBERPUNK.ParryTitle: Declare Parry (block next melee attack, −3 to own other actions)
CYBERPUNK.ParryTitleActive: Parrying (next melee attack blocked) — click to cancel
CYBERPUNK.StillHeldBody: 🤜 {name} is still held by {holder}. Can only attempt Escape this turn.
CYBERPUNK.GrappledReminderBody: 🤜 {name} is grappled by {grappler}. Must escape or the grappler may Hold/Choke/Throw freely.
CYBERPUNK.ExplosionTemplateNotFound: Explosion template not found — it may have been removed.
CYBERPUNK.NoTokensInBlast: No tokens in the blast (or all behind cover).
CYBERPUNK.ScatterBody: 🎲 Scatter — {dir}{drift}. Reposition if needed, then Confirm Blast.
CYBERPUNK.SpreadFireNoToken: Spread fire: the attacker's token isn't on the active scene, so the pattern can't be placed.
CYBERPUNK.SpreadTemplateNotFound: Spread template not found — it may have been removed.
CYBERPUNK.NoTokensInSpread: No tokens in the spread pattern (or all behind cover).
CYBERPUNK.AddActionTitle: Manual Action — mark an action not tracked automatically (e.g., reload, mount/dismount)
CYBERPUNK.DamageSentWaiting: Damage sent — waiting for GM to apply.
CYBERPUNK.LimbWoundDeathSaveClause: Immediate Death Save required at Mortal 0.

Fumble.AutoWeaponOnlyJam: Automatic weapons: fumble table is ignored; only a Reliability jam check is made.
Fumble.DischargeApplied: Result: the weapon discharged (ammo is now 0).
Fumble.DischargeNotApplied: Result: lucky you, it didn’t discharge - just a misfire

AutoNotice.SettingsHint: Open Game Settings → Configure Settings → Cyberpunk 2020: Augmented Edition to review combat options.
AutoNotice.Title: Cyberpunk 2020: Augmented Edition — Setup & What's New
AutoNotice.AblationDesc: Armor SP decreases by 1 per penetrating hit. Permanently modifies armor items.
AutoNotice.CombatItem2: Cover SP field in damage dialog
AutoNotice.CombatItem3: 🎯 Aim, ⏸ Wait, 🛡 Dodge, ⛨ Parry, ➕ Action buttons
AutoNotice.CombatItem4: Stun and death save prompts after damage
AutoNotice.CombatItem11: Automated rangefinding: range category set from token distance — ON by default (toggle in the module settings)
AutoNotice.BeyondItem1: Vehicles & ACPA: drive/pilot, vehicle combat, weapon mounts (+ optional Maximum Metal rules)
AutoNotice.BeyondItem2: Shopping: searchable catalog, GM custom shops, drag an item onto a sheet to buy
AutoNotice.BeyondItem3: Ammunition: buy by caliber + load right from the catalog (box pricing)
AutoNotice.BeyondItem4: Tear-off sheet tabs: hold a tab and drag it into its own window
AutoNotice.BeyondItem5: Gear list: drag to reorder; drag off the sheet to delete
AutoNotice.BeyondItem6: Reputation & Facedown panel on the combat tab
AutoNotice.DeathSaveDesc: Mortal characters are prompted automatically on their turn.
AutoNotice.HeadDoublingDesc: A hit to the Head doubles the FINAL damage, after armor (SP) and BTM resolve (CP2020 p.103). Turn it off in settings if your table skips this rule.
AutoNotice.Intro: This world has Cyberpunk 2020: Augmented Edition installed — a companion module adding combat automation and more on top of the base system. To protect existing characters, most combat automation is OFF by default — enable what your table wants in Game Settings → Configure Settings → Cyberpunk 2020: Augmented Edition.
AutoNotice.LayerEvDesc: Wearing 2+ armor pieces at the same location reduces REF.
AutoNotice.LimbDesc: More than 8 net damage to a limb triggers an immediate Death Save.
AutoNotice.MultiActionDesc: Each action beyond the first pre-fills −3 in the attack modifier dialog.
AutoNotice.OptInHeading: ⚙ Opt-in automation — OFF by default; enable what you want
AutoNotice.StunDesc: Unconscious characters are prompted to recover each turn.

CYBERPUNK.ExplosionConfirmSummary: Blast radius {radius}m, base damage {damage}, full damage within {within}m.
CYBERPUNK.ExplosionConfirmHint: If the throw missed, click Scatter to roll where it really lands; otherwise reposition for cover and click Confirm. Damage falls off by distance.
CYBERPUNK.SpreadConfirmHint: Aim the pattern, then click Confirm. Everyone in the straight path is hit (CP2020 p.108).
CYBERPUNK.DeathSaveAutoResult: ☠ AUTOMATIC DEATH — Death Save threshold is 0 (roll ≤ 0 on 1d10 is impossible). {name} dies.
CYBERPUNK.DeathSaveResultWin: ✅ SURVIVED (rolled {result} ≤ {threshold}) — {name} clings to life. Another save required next turn.
CYBERPUNK.DeathSaveResultLose: ☠ DIED (rolled {result} > {threshold}) — {name} dies at end of this turn. Call Trauma Team.
CYBERPUNK.StabilizeHint: Stabilize: TECH + Medical + 1d10 ≥ total damage ({damage} pts)
CYBERPUNK.TargetPickPrompt: 🎯 Target a token on the canvas:
CYBERPUNK.TargetPickNoneHint: No token targeted yet. Right-click a token → Target, or hover and press T, then click Use Canvas Target.
CYBERPUNK.HeadWoundAutoDeath: ☠ AUTOMATIC DEATH — A head wound of more than 8 points kills automatically (CP2020 p.103). {name} dies. Call Trauma Team.
CYBERPUNK.DeathSaveAutoWarn: ⚠ Automatic death — threshold is 0, no roll can succeed.
CYBERPUNK.DeathSaveNeed: Must roll ≤ {threshold} on 1d10 to survive this turn
CYBERPUNK.SuppPlacementPlaced: The fire zone template has been placed on the canvas.
CYBERPUNK.SuppPlacementHint: Rotate the template to aim, then click Confirm Fire Zone to issue evasion prompts to tokens in the zone. The zone persists until the next round.
CYBERPUNK.StunSaveNeed: Must roll ≤ {threshold} on 1d10 to stay conscious
CYBERPUNK.MartialNoDamageNote: Non-damaging maneuver — resolve the opposed roll with the GM.
CYBERPUNK.MartialFxKnockdownBody: {target} is knocked prone. Must spend one action to stand before acting normally. (CP2020 p.100)
CYBERPUNK.MartialFxHeldBody: {target} is held by {attacker}. The target can only attempt Escape (opposed roll). (CP2020 p.100)
CYBERPUNK.MartialFxGrappledBody: {target} is grappled by {attacker}. The attacker may Hold, Choke, or Throw on later turns as a free action. (CP2020 p.100–102)
CYBERPUNK.MartialFxChokeBody: {target} is being choked by {attacker}. Takes 1d6 damage per turn and must pass a Stun Save each turn or fall unconscious. (CP2020 p.100)
CYBERPUNK.MartialFxEscapedBody: {target} breaks free from the hold / grapple / choke.
CYBERPUNK.SuppEvasionEvaded: ✅ EVADED ({total} > {dc}) — {name} clears the fire zone.
CYBERPUNK.SuppEvasionCaught: ❌ CAUGHT ({total} ≤ {dc}) — {name} takes hits! Rolling damage...
CYBERPUNK.StabilizeResultWin: ✅ STABILIZED — Roll total: {breakdown} ≥ {damage}. No further Death Saves required.
CYBERPUNK.StabilizeResultLose: ❌ FAILED — Roll total: {breakdown} < {damage}. Death Saves continue next turn.
CYBERPUNK.StabilizeResultNote: If new damage is taken, stabilization is lost and Death Saves restart.
CYBERPUNK.StabilizeDialogIntro: TECH + Medical Skill + 1d10 must equal or exceed {damage} total damage.
CYBERPUNK.StabilizeMedLabel: Medical Skill (Medical Tech or First Aid)
CYBERPUNK.StunSaveResultWin: ✅ SUCCESS (rolled {result} ≤ {threshold}) — {name} stays in the fight.
CYBERPUNK.StunSaveRecoveryNote: Can recover by rolling a successful Stun Save on a subsequent turn.
CYBERPUNK.SuppEvasionPromptRoll: Roll Athletics + REF + 1d10 to evade the fire zone.
CYBERPUNK.SuppEvasionPromptHint: REF {ref}, Athletics {ath} — must beat DC {dc} or take hits.
CYBERPUNK.WaitNoOneAfter: {name} is already the last active combatant this round — there is no one to wait after.
CYBERPUNK.WaitingBody: Acting after {target}. A prompt will appear when their turn ends.
CYBERPUNK.DodgeDeclareBody: All incoming melee attacks this round are at −2 to the attacker's roll. Dodge clears at the start of {name}'s next turn. (CP2020 p.102)
CYBERPUNK.ActionPenaltyClause: Penalty: {penalty} to all rolls this round.
CYBERPUNK.YourMomentBody: {name} just acted. Click ⚡ in the tracker to announce your delayed action, then fire from your character sheet.
CYBERPUNK.AcidExpiredBody: ⚗ {name} — Acid effect at {location} expired. Armor SP degradation complete.
CYBERPUNK.GasCloudPlacedBody:
Gas cloud placed on canvas (radius {radius}m). All tokens within the cloud must make Stun Saves each turn (penalty {mod}).
Cloud persists for {duration} turns, then disperses automatically. GM may reposition the template to represent wind drift.
CYBERPUNK.GasCloudProtectedClause: Protected by sealed breathing gear: {names} — no save needed.
CYBERPUNK.GasCloudFilterHeldClause: Filters held for {name} (d10 {roll} ≤ {threshold}) — no save this turn.
CYBERPUNK.GasCloudTurnBody: In the gas cloud: {names}. Each must make a Stun Save{penalty}.
CYBERPUNK.StabilizedLostBody: ⚠ {name} was stabilized but has taken new damage — Death Saves are required again.
CYBERPUNK.ConcussionBody: 💥 {name} — concussion ({weapon}): {permanent} permanent HP (half of {gotThrough} after BTM); the other half is stun. Soft armor −2 SP. Stun Save required.
CYBERPUNK.LimbWoundCoreDetail: {net} net damage — severed or crushed beyond recognition (CP2020 p.103).
CYBERPUNK.MovementLockedAfterAction: Movement is locked: this character has already acted this turn (Restrict Movement to Once per Turn is on).

Vehicle.MMDisabledMissile: Maximum Metal is disabled — enable it in the settings to fire guided missiles.
Vehicle.MissileNeedsTokens: Missile launch needs both the firer and the target on the canvas.
Vehicle.MissileNoCombatHint: ⚠ No active encounter — start one for automatic flight, or advance the missile manually with ▶ in the Missiles in Flight panel (Combat tab).
Vehicle.MissileLaunchedNotice: Missile launched — start an encounter for better missile controls, or advance it manually in the Combat tab's Missiles in Flight panel.
Vehicle.MissileWontArm: ⚠ Fired inside its minimum range — the warhead will not arm; it strikes without detonating.
Vehicle.MissileDudBody: The missile struck {target}, but — fired inside its minimum range — the warhead never armed. No damage.
Vehicle.AntiMissileBurstBody: {weapon} will hit at HALF damage & Penetration.
Vehicle.ReactionAlready: You have already made that reaction to this missile.
Vehicle.NoCmDefeats: No countermeasure defeats this missile's homing method.
Vehicle.MissileRevealTip: Reveal this missile to its target (GM)
Vehicle.DamageDisabled: Vehicle damage automation is disabled in the settings.
Vehicle.NoGMForDamage: No GM is connected to apply damage to {name}.
Vehicle.DamageSentToGM: Vehicle damage sent to the GM ({name}).
Vehicle.NoGMForMissile: No GM is connected to launch the missile.
Vehicle.MissileLaunchSentToGM: Missile launch sent to the GM.
Vehicle.AlreadyOnScene: {name} is already on this scene — resize/move that token.
Vehicle.ChemicalBurstBody: A {radius}m cloud settles over the impact.
Vehicle.IndirectNeedsTarget: Target the impact point (a token at the target's location) before firing indirect.
Vehicle.IndirectIntro: {actor} fires {weapon} indirect at {target}. A spotter corrects fire; on a miss the shell scatters from the aim point.
Vehicle.BombNeedsTarget: Target the aiming point (a token at the target's location) before bombing.
Vehicle.BombIntro: {actor} drops {weapon} on {target}. A direct hit multiplies Penetration ×5; a miss scatters with altitude.
Vehicle.DiveTurnsTip: Turns spent diving at the target; each beyond the first adds +1 to-hit (max +3) and speeds the fall.
Vehicle.AircraftSpeedTip: Power-dive speed; the bomb inherits it (halving each turn to the 175 m/turn floor).
Vehicle.AcpaMeleeOnlyAcpa: ACPA melee is for powered-armor actors (tick the ACPA box).
Vehicle.AcpaNeedTarget: Target the token you're striking first.
Vehicle.RepairOnlyAcpa: Repair is for ACPA (powered armor) only.
Vehicle.AcpaRepaired: {actor} fully repaired (frame SDP, systems & weapons restored).
Vehicle.PilotRefTip: Pilot REF, capped by the suit's Reflex/Control system (derived effective REF). Override if a different pilot is driving.
Vehicle.AcpaHeatstrokeBegins: heat build-up complete — heatstroke begins: pilot Stun/Shock Save at Serious
Vehicle.AcpaHeatstrokeWorsens: heatstroke worsens — pilot Stun/Shock Save at {level}
Vehicle.LuckHitBody: Struck by a Penetration {pen} weapon. Roll LUCK + 1d10 vs 15 (MM p.8).
Vehicle.LuckHitNote: Success = grazed (5D6, half armor). Failure = Penetration vs your Armor Value. LUCK {luck}.
Vehicle.LuckGrazed: GRAZED — {dmg} (5D6) to {loc}, armor at half SP.
Vehicle.LuckStopped: Armor stopped it (Pen {pen} ≤ AV {av}) — {dmg} (2D6) impact to {loc}; armor loses {spStripped} SP.
Vehicle.LuckPenetrated: PENETRATED (Pen {pen} − AV {av} = {diff}) — {dmg} damage to {loc}; armor destroyed.
Vehicle.ControlDisabled: Vehicle control rolls are disabled in the system settings.
Vehicle.ManeuverExamples_simple: swerve, take off / land, hover, rotate, mild turn
Vehicle.ManeuverExamples_difficult: tight turn, control a skid, emergency stop, pull out of a dive, reverse
Vehicle.ManeuverExamples_veryDifficult: bootlegger reverse, extremely tight turn, vertical aerial maneuver, regain control from a spin
Vehicle.ControlFail: ❌ FAILED — control lost (rolled below DV by {missedBy}).
Vehicle.FireNoTargetClause: (no target — apply from the chat card afterward)
Vehicle.ArcWarnStrict: ⛔ Target is to the {bearing} of the firer — outside the {arc} mount's arc. Strict arc blocks this shot: rotate the firing vehicle to face the target (Ctrl+scroll), or use a turret mount.
Vehicle.ArcWarnSoft: ⚠ Target is to the {bearing} of the firer — outside the {arc} mount's arc. You can still fire (override).
Vehicle.ShellTip: Choose the loaded round; the choice is saved on the weapon. Hi-Ex/HEAT shells set their own Penetration, burst and range-immunity.
Vehicle.RofModeTip: This weapon has a variable rate of fire — pick the high or low rate. Sets the ROF field (still editable).
Vehicle.FireControlTip: The vehicle's fire-control / targeting-computer bonus (system.fireControl), auto-applied. Edit to add an ad-hoc bonus on top.
Vehicle.DFBTip: ACPA Direct-Fire Bonus from the suit's Reality Interface (system.dfb), auto-applied when the suit fires its own weapons (replaces a smartgun bonus).
Vehicle.FireApplyHint: No target selected. Target the enemy token (vehicle or person), then:
Vehicle.FireMultiToken: {actor} has multiple tokens on this scene — select the one you're firing from so facing and arc use the right token.
Vehicle.ArcStrictBlock: Strict arc — target is to the {bearing} of the firer, outside the {arc} mount's arc. Rotate to bear or use a turret.
Vehicle.MissileNeedTokensDirect: Missiles need the firer and target on the canvas — resolving as a direct shot.
Vehicle.ApplyTargetOne: Target exactly one token (vehicle or person), then click Apply.
Vehicle.AcpaCheckboxTip: Powered Armor (ACPA). Uncheck to turn this back into a standard vehicle (the sheet switches automatically).
Vehicle.ControlRollTipAcpa: Roll a Control / Maneuver check (REF + Pilot + 1d10 vs Difficulty Value). On failure, rolls the loss/failure table for the active ruleset.
Vehicle.DamageTipAcpa: Resolve damage against this suit. Maximum Metal compares Penetration to Armor Value, then rolls the powered-armor System Hit / Critical Hit / Integrity flow.
Vehicle.MeleeTip: ACPA hand-to-hand (Maximum Metal p.58): Punch / Crush / Kick → vehicle-scale Penetration through the dispatcher. Target a token first.
Vehicle.RepairTip: Field repair: restore the suit to full — frame SDP per area, SDP, power, and clear all damage/status.
Vehicle.ChassisStrTip: Chassis Strength (Linear Frame). Drives Body Value (STR ÷ 20), melee damage and lift (Maximum Metal p.5/61).
Vehicle.BodyValueTip: Body Value = chassis STR ÷ 20 (rounded). Subtracted on the MM damage-severity roll. Read-only.
Vehicle.ArmorSpTip: Armor-shell Stopping Power. ACPA armor is equal on every side (no flank rules); SP ≤ 2× chassis STR (MM p.61).
Vehicle.ArmorValueTipAcpa: Armor Value = SP ÷ 20 (MM p.4). A weapon's Penetration is compared to this. Read-only.
Vehicle.SdpCurrentTip: Structural Damage Points — the suit's structural hit points.
Vehicle.ToughnessTip: Toughness Mod (MM p.62): reduces incoming damage (like BTM). Derived from chassis STR. Read-only.
Vehicle.FrameDamModTip: Standalone-frame melee bonus ONLY — NOT applied to full-ACPA melee, which uses Punch/Crush/Kick (round(STR÷9) d10, MM p.57). This column matters only when the chassis is worn as a bare linear frame (MM p.62-63). Derived.
Vehicle.LiftKgTip: Dead-weight lift (kg), derived from chassis STR.
Vehicle.CarryKgTip: Carry capacity (kg), derived from chassis STR.
Vehicle.FrameSdpNote: Frame SDP per body area (max, from chassis STR — Head/Arm 25%, Leg 50%, Torso 75%). Destroying an area kills its systems; the Torso shuts the suit down.
Vehicle.RealityInterfaceTip: Reality Interface (MM p.64). Always mounted in the helmet. Sets the suit's SIB contribution and its Direct-Fire Bonus (DFB) — the to-hit modifier when the suit fires its own weapons, replacing any smartgun bonus.
Vehicle.ReflexControlTip: Reflex/Control system (MM p.65). Sets the REF modifier and the maximum operating Reflex (the suit's REF cap). Takes no spaces. Advanced is the free military standard.
Vehicle.DfbToHitTip: Direct-Fire Bonus — added to the suit's weapon to-hit rolls (replaces any smartgun bonus). Derived from the Reality Interface. Read-only.
Vehicle.InterfaceSibTip: The Reality Interface's contribution to the suit's Initiative Bonus (SIB). Derived. Read-only.
Vehicle.MaxRefTip: Maximum operating Reflex allowed by the Reflex/Control system. The pilot's REF is capped here while driving the suit. Read-only.
Vehicle.PilotLinkTip: Link the pilot character actor. Its REF drives the suit's effective REF, and it takes frame-breach / overflow damage (firing the normal stun/death saves). Leave as (none) to use the manual Pilot REF.
Vehicle.LinkedPilotRefTip: The linked pilot's base REF (from their character sheet), used as the suit's base REF.
Vehicle.PilotRefManualTip: Manual fallback base Reflex (REF), used when no pilot actor is linked.
Vehicle.EffectiveRefTip: Effective operating REF = clamp(pilot REF + Reflex/Control mod, 0..max) − suit REF critical damage. Derived. Read-only.
Vehicle.CommandComputerTip: Command & Control Computer (C3): squad-coordination option, +1 to Initiative and Awareness while linked. Integrable with any Reflex/Control system.
Vehicle.SibNote: Suit Initiative Bonus (SIB) = round(chassis Lift/Cap ÷ total loaded weight) − 1, plus the Reality Interface SIB. Total weight = chassis + armor shell + Trooper capacity + interface + systems.
Vehicle.SibInitiativeTip: Suit Initiative Bonus (SIB) — added to the pilot's initiative in ACPA combat. Derived from the power-to-weight ratio + the Reality Interface. Read-only.
Vehicle.TotalWtKgTip: Total fully-loaded weight (kg). Derived; drives the SIB. Read-only.
Vehicle.ArmorWtKgTip: Armor-shell weight (kg), from the chosen shell SP via the Armor Inventory Table. Derived. Read-only.
Vehicle.TrooperCapTip: Trooper Capacity — the weight budget set aside for the pilot and personal gear (counts toward total weight for SIB). 114 kg standard; Russian frames 136; elite 80-91.
Vehicle.ExtraSysWtTip: EXTRA / misc systems weight (kg) added ON TOP of the mounted-systems weight (shown in the Systems section). Mounted acpaSystem Items are summed automatically; use this only for gear not in the catalog. Both count toward Total Weight / SIB.
Vehicle.CyberlinkedTip: Cyberlinked controls: +2 to control / maneuver / weapon rolls. ACPA are normally linked.
Vehicle.FireControlTipAcpa: Targeting-computer / fire-control bonus added to weapon to-hit rolls.
Vehicle.DamageControlTipAcpa: Damage Control: a hit system keeps working on a 1d10 of 6-10 (MM p.4).
Vehicle.CompositeArmorTipAcpa: Composite armor: halves shaped-charge (HEAT) Penetration (MM p.23).
Vehicle.AntiMissileTipAcpa: Anti-missile (AGAMS): may shoot down a detected missile.
Vehicle.StrDamageTip: Suit Strength lost to Critical Hits (reduces melee damage and lift). 0 = undamaged.
Vehicle.RefDamageTip: Suit Reflexes lost to Critical Hits (reduces the pilot's REF / control). 0 = undamaged.
Vehicle.PowerHrsTip: Remaining power-cell life in hours (24h normal). Drained by power-unit criticals.
Vehicle.CoolingMinTipAcpa: Minutes until heatstroke after a cooling-system critical (0 = OK). Counts down ~0.05/round (3s rounds); at 0 the heatstroke escalation begins.
Vehicle.HeatstrokeTip: Heatstroke escalation after the cooling build-up expires (MM p.55): 0 none · 1 Serious · 2 Critical · 3 Mortal · 4 out. The pilot makes a Stun/Shock Save one level worse each round.
Vehicle.InterfaceOutTip: Rounds the interface/electronics are out (counts down each combat round).
Vehicle.SeizedRdsTipAcpa: Rounds a body area is seized up / immobile (counts down each round; mobility restored at 0).
Vehicle.WeaponFireTip: Fire this weapon at the target (to-hit roll → resolver)
Vehicle.WeaponAddTip: Create a blank weapon to edit.
Vehicle.WeaponsDragNote: Drag weapons from the Vehicle Weapons (MM) compendium onto this sheet, or add a blank one and edit it.
Vehicle.SystemAddTip: Create a blank system to edit.
Vehicle.SystemsDragNote: Drag systems from the ACPA Systems (MM) compendium onto this sheet, or add a blank one. (Offensive weapons use the Vehicle Weapons (MM) compendium.)
Vehicle.SpacesNote: Spaces used / available per area — internal (enclosed) and external (unprotected). Mounted-systems weight {weight} kg.
Vehicle.SpacesNoteWithCost: Spaces used / available per area — internal (enclosed) and external (unprotected). Mounted-systems weight {weight} kg, cost {cost} eb.
Vehicle.TotalCostTip: Total build cost — chassis + armor shell + Reality Interface + Reflex/Control + Command Computer + systems.
Vehicle.WeightCapTip: Total loaded weight vs the chassis Lift/Capacity. An overweight suit is flagged below.
Vehicle.LegalBuild: ✓ Legal build — within SP, weight, and space limits.
Vehicle.AcpaFootnote: Powered armor (ACPA). The full build system — Linear Frame & Toughness, Reality Interface, Reflex/Control, derived SIB, per-area frame SDP, the systems catalog (per-system SDP), and the point-buy budget validator — and combat (Penetration damage, the System Hit / Critical Hit chart, melee) are in place.
Vehicle.AcpaPoweredArmorTip: Powered Armor (ACPA): derives Body Value from chassis STR ÷ 20 instead of SDP, has equal armor on every facing, takes no size penalty to be hit, and includes a built-in vehicle link (Maximum Metal, p.52).
Vehicle.ControlRollTipVehicle: Roll a Control / Maneuver check (REF + Driving/Pilot + 1d10 vs Difficulty Value). On failure, rolls the Control Loss / Failure table for the active ruleset.
Vehicle.DamageTipVehicle: Resolve damage against this vehicle. Core subtracts SP and reduces SDP; Maximum Metal compares Penetration to Armor Value, rolls Surface/Minor/Major/Catastrophic and a hit location with fuel-fire / ammo-cookoff / crew effects.
Vehicle.SpFrontTip: Stopping Power — the vehicle's armor. Core subtracts SP from incoming damage (p.112); Maximum Metal instead converts it to Armor Value (SP ÷ 20).
Vehicle.AvFrontTip: Armor Value = SP ÷ 20 (Maximum Metal, p.4). A weapon's Penetration is compared to this value — not to raw SP — to see if it gets through. Read-only; edit SP to change it.
Vehicle.FacingSpTip: Per-facing armor (Maximum Metal flank rules, p.4): a hit to the side uses 75% of armor, top/rear/bottom 50%. The facing depends on the angle of the shot. ACPA armor is equal on all sides.
Vehicle.SdpCurrentVehicleTip: Structural Damage Points — the vehicle's hit points. Damage that gets past SP comes off here; at 0 SDP the vehicle is destroyed / inoperable (Core p.112).
Vehicle.SdpMaxVehicleTip: Maximum SDP — the undamaged total. Body Value is derived from this.
Vehicle.BodyValueVehicleTip: Body Value = SDP ÷ 20 (rounded), or chassis STR ÷ 20 for ACPA. The vehicle's bulk/toughness on the Maximum Metal damage scale (p.4): it is subtracted on the damage-severity roll. Every vehicle has one (e.g. SDP 50 → Body 3); Core mode just doesn't use it — it tracks raw SDP instead. Read-only.
Vehicle.ChassisStrVehicleTip: ACPA chassis Strength. For powered armor, Body Value is STR ÷ 20 instead of SDP ÷ 20 (Maximum Metal, p.5).
Vehicle.CoolingMinTipVehicle: Minutes until heatstroke after a cooling-system critical (0 = OK). When it expires the pilot makes escalating Stun Saves.
Vehicle.SeizedRdsTipVehicle: Rounds a body area is seized up / immobile (counts down each combat round; restores mobility at 0).
Vehicle.TopSpeedTip: Maximum speed. In Maximum Metal, going fast costs handling: the maneuver Difficulty rises +1 for every 10% of top speed you are driving above 50% of top speed (p.11).
Vehicle.SafeSpeedTip: The fastest you can go without a handling penalty. In Core, driving at 2× / 3× / 4× safe speed applies −2 / −4 / −6 to the Control Roll (p.112).
Vehicle.AccelerationTip: How much speed the vehicle can gain per combat turn (Maximum Metal chase rules).
Vehicle.DecelerationTip: How much speed the vehicle can shed per combat turn — braking (Maximum Metal chase rules).
Vehicle.ControlModTip: Vehicle handling modifier added to every Control / Maneuver roll (e.g. sportscar +2, limo −3, truck −4). It prefills the Control Roll dialog. Core uses the p.112 table; Maximum Metal the revised p.11 table.
Vehicle.CrewSlotsTip: How many crew positions the vehicle has (driver, gunner, etc.). At least one is needed to operate it.
Vehicle.PassengerSlotsTip: How many additional passengers the vehicle can carry beyond the crew.
Vehicle.VehicleLinkTip: Cybernetic vehicle link: the driver/pilot adds +2 to all control, maneuver, and vehicle-weapon rolls, and can act while driving without the 'doing something else' penalty (Maximum Metal). ACPA always counts as linked.
Vehicle.DamageControlTip: A redundant network of backups, fire extinguishers and breakers. When a system is hit, roll 1d10 — on a 6-10 it keeps working as if undamaged (Maximum Metal, p.4). Used by the damage resolver.
Vehicle.CompositeArmorTip: Composite armor (metals + plastics + ceramics + depleted heavy metals): halves the Penetration of shaped-charge HEAT weapons, but penetrating damage is not halved (Maximum Metal, p.23). Used by the damage resolver.
Vehicle.ReactiveArmorTip: Reactive armor: explosive tiles that detonate against shaped-charge (HEAT) attacks. On a HEAT hit, roll 1d10 — 2-10 halves Penetration, minus 1 per two prior shaped-charge / high-explosive hits the array has absorbed. Tiles are consumed each hit; press Replace to rearm after a battle (Maximum Metal, p.23). Stacks with Composite (¼ Pen if both fire). Used by the damage resolver.
Vehicle.ReactiveWearTip: Shaped-charge / high-explosive hits absorbed by the reactive tiles. The deflection roll drops 1 for every two hits. Replace rearms the array (resets wear to 0).
Vehicle.ReactiveReplaceTip: Rearm the reactive array (reset wear to 0)
Vehicle.SensorsRadarTip: Sensors / radar: automatically detect an inbound missile (90%) so the crew can react. Without sensors, detection falls back to a Notice/Awareness roll (Maximum Metal, p.10/25-26).
Vehicle.AntiMissileTip: Anti-missile system (AGAMS/AEAMS): when a missile is detected, the crew may attempt to shoot it down — destroy on a roll of 4+, detonate it early (half damage) on 1-3 (Maximum Metal, p.24).
Vehicle.CountermeasuresHint: Countermeasures the vehicle carries (MM p.9-10). An incoming missile is opposed by the best +Difficulty a carried countermeasure imposes on its homing method.
Vehicle.FireControlTipVehicle: Targeting-computer bonus added to the vehicle's weapon to-hit rolls (Maximum Metal). 0 = no fire-control computer.
Vehicle.VehicleFootnote: Set the actor image (top-left) to your vehicle art, then drag the actor onto the canvas like any other actor — the token is pre-sized and scales to fit (drag its resize handle to adjust). Crew tokens placed on top render above it and ride along once boarded. Weapon mounts and the damage resolver arrive in later phases.
Vehicle.SysBodyAreaTip: Body area this system is mounted in. Destroying that area's frame knocks out its systems (Maximum Metal p.61).
Vehicle.SysMountTip: Internal = enclosed, protected by the suit armor. External = NOT armor-protected (carries its own SP). Either / Retract per the catalog.
Vehicle.SysSpacesTip: Spaces this system consumes in its body area (internal or external space budget, Maximum Metal p.61).
Vehicle.SysSPTip: Intrinsic Stopping Power (external / retractable items have their own SP).
Vehicle.SysSDPTip: Structural points — the system is destroyed when its accumulated SDP damage reaches this. 0 = derive 3× SP (Maximum Metal p.62).
Vehicle.SysWeightKgTip: Weight (kg) — counts toward total loaded weight for the SIB.
Vehicle.SysCostEbTip: Cost (eb) — counts toward the build budget.
Vehicle.SysSDPDamageTip: Accumulated SDP damage from hits to this system (0 = undamaged).
Vehicle.SysDestroyedTip: This system is destroyed (its SDP was exceeded). Set automatically by the damage resolver; editable for field repairs.
Vehicle.WClassTip: Resolution archetype. Direct-fire = guns; Burst = HE/HEAT shells; Rocket = unguided HE; Missile = guided; Artillery = indirect; Bomb; Cone = scatter-packs; Melee = ACPA; Special.
Vehicle.WMountTip: Weapon mount (MM p.15). Turret = 360°/+2 to-hit; Fixed = forward only; Articulated = one side; Open/Pintle = exposed; Pod = exterior, fixed forward, swappable; Jury-rigged = WA-2, one direction, exposed (max 1 space).
Vehicle.WArcTip: Firing arc relative to the vehicle's facing.
Vehicle.WATip: Weapon Accuracy: a flat modifier to the to-hit roll (MM stat block).
Vehicle.PenetrationTip: Vehicle Penetration — the parenthetical (n) in the MM stat block. Given directly by the book; compared to the target's Armor Value.
Vehicle.DamageDiceTip: Personnel damage dice (e.g. 9D10). Used for the MM p.8 alternative method vs characters.
Vehicle.FlagAPTip: Armor-piercing: ×2 Penetration Factor (already baked into the listed Pen for AP weapons).
Vehicle.FlagHEATTip: HEAT / shaped-charge: Penetration is NOT affected by range; Composite Armor halves Pen (penetrating damage is not halved).
Vehicle.FlagHiExTip: High-explosive: Penetration is not affected by range.
Vehicle.HiDensityAPTip: High-density AP (errata p.105): full damage through armor, like HEAT (e.g. Barret 20mm, 30mm Gatling).
Vehicle.BurstMTip: Burst radius in meters (0 = no area effect). Hi-Ex/HEAT shells, rockets, GLs, mortars, bombs.
Vehicle.ROFTip: Rate of fire. ROF 30 hits with 5 rounds per hit, ROF 100 with 10 (extra rounds add Penetration).
Vehicle.ROFAltTip: Alternate ROF for variable weapons (e.g. '30 OR 5'). 0 = none.
Vehicle.MinRangeTip: Minimum range in meters (missiles = 1/10 Long range; 0 = none).
Vehicle.GuidanceTip: Missile guidance: Semi-active uses the operator's skill; Active uses the missile's own Skill (ignores operator); Paint hits 2-10 if a painting laser hits.
Vehicle.GuideSkillTip: Active missile's own Skill rating (e.g. +15 / +20), used in place of the operator's skill.
Vehicle.HomingTip: Homing method — determines which countermeasures defeat it (radar ← chaff/jamming/ECM; thermal/IR ← flares/IR-baffling/IR-smoke; optical ← smoke; laser ← anti-laser aerosol).
Vehicle.ConeAngleTip: Cone angle in degrees for scatter-pack weapons (60 / 120 / 180).
Vehicle.ProjectilesTip: Number of projectiles per volley (cone/scatter weapons).
Vehicle.ScatterDiceTip: Scatter-pack munition dice (MM p.72): XD6 rolled per hit target for how many munitions strike (applied as multiple rounds). 0 = not a scatter-pack.
Vehicle.ShellVariantsHint: Offered when firing. The warhead drives the effect: HEAT (shaped charge, halved by composite), WP (3D6 burn), Cluster (×3 burst, Pen 4), Chemical (gas cloud, no Pen). Leave empty for a plain HE round.
Vehicle.LocomotionTip: Movement class — drives the aircraft loss-table branch (stall/spin vs skid/roll). Set this, not the type name.

CYBERPUNK.MechLightColorHint: Optional tint as #rrggbb — leave empty for plain white light.
CYBERPUNK.MechVisionRequiresHint: Illuminator dependency: the device only works while the actor carries one of these items, equipped and switched on. Exact item names, several separated by | — leave empty for a self-sufficient device.
CYBERPUNK.MechProtectionPercentHint: Percent-effective gear (e.g. nasal filters "70% effective"): each exposure rolls a d10 and is protected on a roll at or under this ÷ 10. 0 = not percent-gated.
CYBERPUNK.MechProtectionDamageMultHint: Damage multiplier for this hazard (e.g. "−25% from SW" = 0.75). Applied where a hazard damage path exists; sonic has none yet (data-ready). 0 = none.
CYBERPUNK.MechRollModsAutoHint: Suggest this bonus already checked in the roll dialog. Untick for gear that only helps in narrow situations (e.g. a decryptor that only works on one lock type).
CYBERPUNK.MechConsumableDurationHint: Blank = no timer. A number or roll formula (e.g. 1d6+2), rolled when used; the effect wears off after that many of the user's combat turns.
CYBERPUNK.ConsumableExpiredBody: {name}{noteClause} wears off — {actor} is no longer under its effect.
CYBERPUNK.VisionPickHint: Which vision device governs your sight. Auto = the longest-range active device; Natural suspends device overrides.
CYBERPUNK.LivingFlagHint: Heat-sensing optics (thermograph / IR) reveal this actor's token. Characters and NPCs default on; machines default off.
CYBERPUNK.ChipChooseForCategory: Choose a {category} skill for this chip (pick one or type a name):
CYBERPUNK.ChipChooseAny: Choose a skill for this chip (pick one or type a name):
CYBERPUNK.ChipChoiceResetHint: Clear the chosen skill so the chip can be slotted for a different one.
CYBERPUNK.MechContainerInstalledInHint: Install this item into a container (a limb compartment, a skin pouch, another container) with free slots. Number in parentheses = slots free.
CYBERPUNK.ManeuverSpeedFull: Maneuver (safe) speed — the fastest speed that needs no control roll. Not the Maneuverability modifier.
CYBERPUNK.ManeuverabilityHint: Maneuverability (MV) — the vehicle's control-roll modifier. Not a speed.
CYBERPUNK.BodyRatingFull: Body rating — the abstract structural class printed beside SDP (e.g. "SDP 100 (Body 5)")
CYBERPUNK.IpTrackerFooter: Queue rows come from skill rolls. Enter IP (or tick success), then Apply to release pending IP to players. Pending IP is hidden from players until applied.
CYBERPUNK.IpRawOffNote: RAW auto-tracking is off, so skill rolls don't queue here. Turn on "Improvement Points: RAW auto-tracking" in settings to collect rolls automatically, or use ＋ Add to grant IP by hand.
CYBERPUNK.IpBalancesEmpty: No IP banked or pending yet.
CYBERPUNK.IpPoolHint: The actor's fungible IP pool — spendable on any skill. Editable: type an absolute value to add or correct.
CYBERPUNK.IpBankHint: IP banked to this skill. Editable: type an absolute value to add or remove (fixes a mis-award).
CYBERPUNK.IpQueueOverflow: The Improvement-Point roll log is full ({max} rolls); the oldest un-awarded rolls are now aging out. Apply or clear the IP Tracker to keep them.
CYBERPUNK.IpQueueOverflowGm: IP roll log full ({max}) — oldest un-awarded rolls are aging out. Open the IP Tracker to Apply or clear.
CYBERPUNK.IpQueueEmpty: No skill rolls waiting. Rolls appear here as players use skills.
CYBERPUNK.IpThrottleReset: IP throttle reset for a new cycle.
CYBERPUNK.IpManualAddTip: Manually add IP to a skill (or the pool).
CYBERPUNK.IpThrottleResetTip: Reset the per-skill throttle counters for a fresh cycle.
CYBERPUNK.IpApplyTip: Award all queued rows and release pending IP to players. Starts a new cycle.
CYBERPUNK.IpSuccessTip: Mark this use a success → grants the baseline IP.
CYBERPUNK.IpLockGmOnly: Only the GM can change the skill lock in this mode.
CYBERPUNK.IpPendingPip: Pending IP (GM only, hidden from players until applied)
CYBERPUNK.IpNeglectBody: RAW Improvement-Point auto-tracking is on, and {count} skill rolls are queued un-awarded.
CYBERPUNK.IpNeglectQuestion: Do you want to keep using it?
CYBERPUNK.PresetIntro: Pick a playstyle and apply its whole rules-and-automation suite in one click. Each tier builds on the one before it. You can fine-tune any individual setting afterward, and undo the last apply below.
CYBERPUNK.PresetUntouchedNote: Presets never change your permissions, personal display preferences, or content/pricing choices.
CYBERPUNK.PresetManualDesc: The system is just your sheet and dice for combat — you run the fight by hand, with all combat automation off. Shopping and Improvement-Point tracking stay on (both are easy to ignore if your table doesn't use them).
CYBERPUNK.PresetStandardDesc: Automated core combat plus vehicles: damage application, saves, dodge/parry, weapon effects, and vehicle combat. (Shopping and Improvement-Point tracking are on in every tier.)
CYBERPUNK.PresetByTheBookDesc: Strict core rules-as-written: everything in Standard, plus armor ablation and once-per-turn movement. (Shopping and the RAW Improvement-Point system are on in every tier.)
CYBERPUNK.PresetMaximumCrunchDesc: By the Book plus the supplement layer: Maximum Metal vehicles, Listen Up limb crippling and detailed explosives, and Friday Night Firefight 2.
CYBERPUNK.PresetApplied: Preset applied: {tier}. Use Undo in the Presets window to revert.
CYBERPUNK.PresetAlreadyActive: {tier} is already the active configuration — nothing to change.
CYBERPUNK.PresetFeatureRawIp: RAW Improvement-Point auto-tracking (accrues in the background)

CYBERPUNK.AmmoBuyFailed: Could not stock the ammunition; your eurobucks were refunded.
CYBERPUNK.AmmoBuyInsufficientFunds: Not enough eurobucks: a box costs {cost}eb but you only have {funds}eb.

CYBERPUNK.AmmoRoundsPerBox: Rounds per box (the buy quantity is in boxes)

CYBERPUNK.CatalogBooksEyeHint: Click a book's eye to show or hide its items from players. The filters here only change your own view.

CYBERPUNK.ShopAlreadyStocked: That item is already in the shop.

CYBERPUNK.ShopBulkAddConfirm: Add {n} items to this shop? That's everything currently shown — search or filter the list first if you only want some.
CYBERPUNK.ShopBulkAddHint: Add every item currently listed below to this shop (search/filter first to narrow it).
CYBERPUNK.ShopBulkNone: Everything currently shown is already stocked.
CYBERPUNK.ShopBuyFailed: Purchase failed — you were not charged.
CYBERPUNK.ShopBuyerNeeded: Select a token or assign yourself a character to buy for.
CYBERPUNK.ShopBuyerPickHint: Choose which of your characters is shopping.
CYBERPUNK.ShopClearVendorConfirm: Remove all {n} item(s) from this shop? This can't be undone.
CYBERPUNK.ShopClearVendorHint: Remove every item from this shop's stock.
CYBERPUNK.ShopCtxHint: Click to open · right-click for options
CYBERPUNK.ShopDeleteConfirm: Delete this shop? This can't be undone.
CYBERPUNK.ShopDisabled: Shopping is disabled — ask your GM to enable it.
CYBERPUNK.ShopDiscountHint: Shop-wide % off — adjust live (e.g. when players win a persuasion roll).
CYBERPUNK.ShopFullSearchHint: Let players search & buy the whole (allowed) catalog here, with your curated items featured. Off = players see only the items you've added.

CYBERPUNK.ShopVendorEmpty: Drag items here, or ＋ from the list below.

CYBERPUNK.AllOutParryHint: FNFF2: commit your whole turn to parrying for a strong defensive bonus — you can't attack.

ArmorLayers.CoreInfoTip: Hard (H) = rigid: MetalGear, Body Armor, Bodyplating. Max 1 per location. SW = Skinweave (no EV penalty).

ArmorLayers.MaxLayersTip: More than 3 layers — violates New Rule 1

CYBERPUNK.BlockParryHint: Defensive: block or parry an incoming melee attack outright — no damage if you win the contest. Blades must save or break. -3 to your actions while blocking.
CYBERPUNK.CastHint: FNFF2: a throwing maneuver — hurl the opponent using their own momentum.
CYBERPUNK.CatalogHiddenDivider: ⌄ Hidden from players (enable the source to sell these)
CYBERPUNK.CatalogHomebrewOff: Enable “Allow homebrew content” in System Settings to use these.
CYBERPUNK.CatalogSourcesHint: Tick a book to let players buy its items. You always see everything; disabled books show dimmed. This sets catalog-wide visibility — stock each shop separately.
CYBERPUNK.CatalogSupplementsHint: Tick to include a source book in the catalog players see. Hidden items still show here (dimmed).
CYBERPUNK.ChokeHint: Strangle a held target: 1D6 damage per turn, and they must pass a Stun Save each turn or fall unconscious.
CYBERPUNK.CyberInstallTip: Pay surgery cost, roll Humanity loss, and apply surgical damage to the owner, then mark this installed.
CYBERPUNK.DisarmHint: Knock the target's weapon out of their hands on a successful contested roll.
CYBERPUNK.DodgeHint: Defensive: incoming melee attacks take -2 to hit this turn. You're at -3 to your own actions while dodging.
CYBERPUNK.DuplicateSkillAdded: This character already has a skill named ‘{name}’. Added another — some skills (e.g. Play Instrument, Teaching) are taken multiple times; rename it to keep them distinct.
CYBERPUNK.EscapeHint: Break free from an opponent's Hold or Grapple on a successful contested roll.
CYBERPUNK.FacedownHint: Roll 1d10 + COOL + Reputation. Target one foe's token first to make it a contested Facedown.
CYBERPUNK.FacedownPenalty: {loser} backs down — or is at −3 vs {winner} until they win a Facedown against them. (GM enforces.)
CYBERPUNK.FacedownSoloHint: Target one foe's token to make this a contested Facedown.
CYBERPUNK.FacedownTie: Tie — neither side backs down; no penalty.
CYBERPUNK.FireModeNotCapable: This weapon isn't capable of that fire mode.
CYBERPUNK.GrappleHint: Grab the target. While grappled you can Hold, Choke, or Throw them as a free action on later turns.
CYBERPUNK.HoldHint: Immobilize a grabbed target until they break free with Escape.
CYBERPUNK.JumpKickHint: FNFF2: leaping kick — higher damage than a standard kick.
CYBERPUNK.KickHint: Kick attack — 1D6 + STR damage bonus + martial-art bonus. Hits harder than a strike. Contested.
CYBERPUNK.LimbWoundLuCrippledDetail: {net} net damage to {limb} — crippled and cannot be used until repaired/healed (Listen Up, Crippling Injuries).
CYBERPUNK.LimbWoundLuDestroyedDetail: {net} net damage to {limb} — useless, blown off or shredded; needs replacement (Listen Up, Crippling Injuries).
CYBERPUNK.LimbWoundW4DisabledDetail: {net} net damage — disabled (more than 8) (W4RST4R's Limb Rules).
CYBERPUNK.LimbWoundW4SeveredDetail: {net} net damage — severed or crushed beyond recognition (more than 12) (W4RST4R's Limb Rules).
CYBERPUNK.MartialArtBonusesHint: Per-action bonuses for this style. Used when the skill is selected as the Martial Art for an attack.

CYBERPUNK.MartialFxThrowBody: {target} is knocked prone by Throw. Must spend one action to stand up before acting normally. (CP2020 p.100)
CYBERPUNK.MigrationBegin: Cyberpunk2020 System Migration to version {version} starting — please wait…

CYBERPUNK.NoAmmoInventory: Cannot fire: no ammunition in inventory. Add/link ammo, or turn off Ammo Tracking (Free Fire).
CYBERPUNK.NoLinkedAmmo: No linked ammunition in inventory to reload from. Link ammo on the weapon, or turn off Ammo Tracking (Free Fire).
CYBERPUNK.PunchHint: Basic punch — 1D3 + STR damage bonus + martial-art bonus. Contested melee strike.
CYBERPUNK.RamHint: FNFF2: charging body-slam — drive into the target with your momentum.
CYBERPUNK.RecognitionHint: Roll 1d10 vs your Reputation: at or under = they recognize you; over = they haven't heard of you.
CYBERPUNK.RecognizedNo: Not recognized — rolled {roll} > Rep {rep}. They haven't heard of {name}.
CYBERPUNK.ReputationHint: Your social standing (CP2020 p.54). GM-set; can be negative for cowardice/infamy. Feeds the Facedown and Recognition rolls.
CYBERPUNK.ServiceAddTip: Add a recurring service to track.

SETTINGS.AugmentedCombatAutomationHint: Enable automated damage application, saving throws, and area effects for the Augmented Edition combat features. Off by default.
SETTINGS.AutomationNoticeHide: Hide the Setup / What's New notice

SETTINGS.RestrictMovementOncePerTurnHint: When enabled, during an active combat a combatant may reposition freely until they take a tracked action this turn (such as firing a weapon); after acting, further movement is blocked until the next round (CP2020 p.99: one move plus one action per turn). The GM can always move tokens (override). Off by default.
SETTINGS.FNFF2EnabledHint: Enable the expanded Friday Night Fistfight 2 martial-arts ruleset — extra styles, actions, and the per-action damage-bonus rules used by the Augmented martial panel. Hidden when the underlying system already provides this toggle (that one is used instead). Off by default.
SETTINGS.AutoRangefindingHint: When enabled and exactly one target is selected, the attack dialog pre-selects the range band from the measured token distance. Hidden when the underlying system already provides this toggle. On by default.
SETTINGS.SpecialMeleeEffectsHint: When enabled, a successful Hold/Grapple sets a status flag on the target with a reminder card; Choke sets a 1d6/turn flag; Throw/Sweep post a knockdown announcement; Escape clears the hold/grapple/choke flags (CP2020 p.100–102). Hidden when the underlying system already provides this toggle. On by default.
SETTINGS.IpRawTrackingHint: Improvement Points are always available as a simple pool you can ignore. Turn this ON for the full RAW per-skill system: skill rolls are auto-queued for GM-attributed awards, IP banks per skill, and players level up from the skill's own bank (then the pool). When on, the award-model / auto-baseline / throttle / skill-lock options below apply. Off by default.
SETTINGS.IpHideUI: Improvement Points: Hide the IP UI
SETTINGS.IpHideUIHint: Hide the Improvement Points UI entirely — IP buttons, the pool, and the tracker — for pure-narrative tables that don't track advancement with IP. Stored IP is kept, just not shown. Off by default.
SETTINGS.IpAwardModelHint: Manual = the GM types IP per skill use (RAW). Auto baseline = the GM ticks success on a queued roll and a fixed IP is granted automatically (the GM can still add bonus IP).
SETTINGS.IpAutoBaselineAmountHint: IP granted per GM-marked success when the award model is Auto baseline.
SETTINGS.IpThrottleHint: Optional secondary cap on farming one skill. Off = GM polices manually (default). Hard cap = one award per skill per Apply cycle. Diminishing = each further award on the same skill this cycle is halved.
SETTINGS.IpSkillLockModeHint: Who controls the skill-lock that prevents hand-editing skill levels/IP (rolling and confirm-button level-ups always work). Owner = the player. GM = the GM only. Mutual = a player soft-lock plus a GM hard-lock the player can't override.
SETTINGS.IpShowPendingHint: Show the GM-only “pending IP” pip on skill rows (pending IP is hidden from players until applied). Per-user.
SETTINGS.PresetMenuHint: Set up the whole rules-and-automation suite in one click by picking a playstyle tier (Manual, Standard, By the Book, or Maximum Crunch), instead of toggling each setting by hand.
SETTINGS.ShopShowSourceHint: Show a small badge on each shop item naming the book/source it comes from. Per-user — turn it off if you prefer cleaner rows.
SETTINGS.ShoppingEnabledHint: Master toggle for the Shopping feature. When on, a 'Shop' button appears on character sheets and shops/purchases become available. Off by default (opt-in).
SETTINGS.PlayersCanShopHint: When off, only the GM can buy; players are directed to ask the GM. Keeps gear from appearing out of thin air.
SETTINGS.ShopAllowHomebrewHint: Off by default. When on, community / non-canon / cross-system items become available in the shop, where the GM can enable individual sources for players. Core and official R.Talsorian books are unaffected by this.
SETTINGS.ShopBuySourceHint: What players can buy from when buying directly: the full catalog (all compendia), or only shops the GM has published. Under published-only, players can still browse the catalog but must request GM permission to buy from it.
SETTINGS.ShopShowSource: Show item sources in the shop
SETTINGS.PlayersCanBuyAmmoHint: When off, only the GM can buy ammo; players are told they must purchase it at a shop and should ask the GM. Keeps ammo from appearing out of thin air.
SETTINGS.CarolingianSkinHint: Give the character and item sheets a dark cyberpunk-terminal look (teal accents, bracketed tabs, prompt-style headers), adapted from DARKNEET's Cyberpunk Restyler and the Carolingian UI palette. Per-user; on by default. Turn it off for the standard light/dark sheet theme. Install the Carolingian UI module for more colour palettes and a matching interface theme.
SETTINGS.DamageArmorModeHint: How armor SP is applied when calculating damage. Full = SP + ablation per RAW. Simple = SP subtracted, no ablation. None = armor ignored (BTM still applies).
SETTINGS.DamageAblationHint: When enabled, armor SP at the hit location is reduced by 1 for each penetrating hit (RAW).
SETTINGS.DamageAutoApplyHint: When enabled, damage is applied to the target immediately when a targeted weapon fires, without showing the confirmation dialog.
SETTINGS.HeadHitDoublingHint: When enabled, a hit to the Head doubles the FINAL damage — after armor (SP) and BTM are applied. RAW: 'A head hit always doubles damage' (CP2020 p.103, the optional 'He Shrugs Off Head Hits' rule); the book gives no timing, so the wound that actually gets through is what doubles. Disable for groups that skip this rule.
SETTINGS.LimbLossEnabled: Combat: Limb Loss & Head Wound Checks
SETTINGS.LimbLossEnabledHint: When enabled, a single hit dealing more than 8 net damage to a limb triggers an immediate Death Save at Mortal 0 (severed/crushed). A head wound of the same severity kills automatically (CP2020 p.103 RAW).
SETTINGS.SuppressiveFireSavesHint: When enabled, suppressive fire automatically places a ray template (fire zone) on the canvas and prompts all tokens within it to roll an Evasion check: Athletics + REF + 1d10 vs DC = rounds / zone width (CP2020 p.101 RAW). Failures take 1d6 random hits.
SETTINGS.AutoDeathSavePerTurnHint: When enabled, unstabilized Mortal characters are automatically prompted to make a Death Save at the start of each of their turns in the combat tracker (CP2020 p.105 RAW).
SETTINGS.AutoSaveRePrompt: Combat: Stun Save Recovery Each Turn
SETTINGS.AutoSaveRePromptHint: When enabled, unconscious/stunned characters are automatically prompted to roll a Stun Save recovery check at the start of each of their turns in the combat tracker (CP2020 p.104 RAW).
SETTINGS.ActiveDodgeParryEnabledHint: When enabled, 🛡 Dodge and ⛨ Parry buttons appear in the combat tracker. Dodge (active combatant): −2 to attacker's melee roll this round; clears on next turn. Parry (any combatant, reactive): blocks the next incoming melee attack; consumed on use. (CP2020 p.102 RAW.)
SETTINGS.AimTrackingEnabledHint: When enabled, a Take Aim (🎯) button appears in the combat tracker for the active combatant. Each click accumulates +1 aim round (max 3) stored on the actor. The attack modifier dialog is automatically pre-filled with the saved aim count. Aim resets when the actor fires. (CP2020 p.99 RAW: +1 per consecutive aim round, max +3.)
SETTINGS.WaitForTurnEnabledHint: When enabled, a Wait (⏸) button appears in the combat tracker for the active combatant. Clicking it sets their initiative just below the current minimum and advances to the next combatant, so they act last this round. Since CP2020 re-rolls initiative each round, this is a temporary deferral. (CP2020 p.98 RAW.)
SETTINGS.SpecialMeleeEffectsEnabled: Combat: Martial Arts Special Hit Effects
SETTINGS.SpecialMeleeEffectsEnabledHint: When enabled, successful Hold/Grapple attacks set a status flag on the target with turn-start reminders; Choke deals 1d6 HP damage per turn + forces a Stun Save; Throw/Sweep post knockdown announcements; Escape removes all hold/grapple/choke flags. (CP2020 p.100–102 RAW.)
SETTINGS.GasGrenadeCloudEnabled: Combat: Gas Grenade Cloud & Per-Turn Saves
SETTINGS.GasGrenadeCloudEnabledHint: When enabled, weapons loaded with gas ammo (effectTypes: ['Gas'] on ammo item) place a green circle MeasuredTemplate on the canvas. All tokens within the cloud are prompted to make Stun Saves each turn. Cloud persists for dotTurns turns then auto-deletes. (CP2020 p.107 area weapon rules.)
SETTINGS.GasCloudAutoMoveHint: When enabled, the gas cloud template drifts 2m in a random direction each turn to simulate wind movement (CP2020 p.107). When disabled, the GM may reposition the template manually.
SETTINGS.TaserCumPenaltyEnabledHint: When enabled, each successive taser hit within a 3-turn window reduces the target's Stun Save threshold by the ammo item's stunSaveMod value (default −2 per hit). The penalty accumulates: 2nd hit −2, 3rd hit −4, etc. (CP2020 p.101 RAW.)
SETTINGS.AcidArmorDotEnabledHint: When enabled, weapons loaded with acid ammo (dotEnabled on ammo item) degrade the target's armor SP at the hit location by the dotDamageFormula roll (default 1d6) per turn for dotTurns turns. SP is reduced from the outermost layer inward. (CP2020 acid weapon rules.)
SETTINGS.AcidDotStackModeHint: Controls what happens when a target is hit by acid while an acid effect is already active. Stack: extends the remaining turns at the same location. Reset: overwrites the previous effect (timer restarts). Separate: both effects run concurrently with independent timers.
SETTINGS.FireDotEnabledHint: When enabled, weapons loaded with incendiary/API ammo set the target on fire: the dotDamageFormula roll (default 1d6) is applied as HP damage at the hit location each turn for dotTurns turns, with a Stun Save each turn. Unlike acid, fire burns the target, not their armor.
SETTINGS.FireDotStackModeHint: Controls what happens when a target is set on fire while already burning. Stack: extends the remaining turns at the same location. Reset: overwrites the previous fire (timer restarts). Separate: both fires run concurrently with independent timers.
SETTINGS.MultiActionPenaltyEnabledHint: When enabled, each action taken beyond the first in a round applies a cumulative −3 penalty to all rolls that round. A badge in the combat tracker shows the current action count and live penalty. (CP2020 p.105 RAW.)
SETTINGS.MultiActionAutoTrackHint: When enabled, weapon fire and tracker button clicks (Aim, Dodge, Parry) automatically increment the action counter. When disabled, only the manual ➕ button in the tracker changes the count — useful for tables that prefer full manual control.
SETTINGS.ShotgunSpreadEnabledHint: When enabled, ammo whose Spread Mode is not 'single' (buckshot, flechette) fires a widening pattern (Close 1m/Med 2m/Long 3m by default) with range-banded damage. Everyone in the straight path takes the hit. Only affects ammo explicitly configured for spread, so normal weapons are unchanged. (CP2020 p.108.)
SETTINGS.ExplosivesEnabledHint: When enabled, ammo whose Effect Types include 'Explosive' detonates as an area-effect blast: a circular zone of radius blastRadius, with range-banded damage falloff (blastMultipliers) outward from the center. Every token in the blast takes damage through the normal pipeline. Only affects ammo configured as Explosive. (CP2020 p.108.)
SETTINGS.AreaEffectOcclusionHint: When enabled, a token shielded by a wall between it and the blast center (or the shooter, for spread) is exempt from area-effect damage — intervening cover blocks the pattern/blast (CP2020 p.108). Requires walls placed on the scene; disable if your tables don't map cover with walls.
SETTINGS.ExplosivesDetailed: Combat: Detailed Explosives — HEP Concussion (Listen Up)
SETTINGS.ExplosivesDetailedHint: Optional grittier blast model from Listen Up You Primitive Screwheads (p.105). Explosion concussion is treated as HEP: armor SP does NOT protect (BTM still applies), half the damage that gets through is permanent and half is stun (a Stun Save is always prompted), and soft armor at the hit location loses 2 SP. If the ammo also has blastShrapnel, each target additionally takes a normal-armor 1d10 shrapnel hit. Default OFF (Core blast = damage through normal armor).
SETTINGS.LimbModelHint: Which limb-injury rules apply when a hit lands on an arm or leg (requires 'Limb Loss & Head Wound Checks'). Core: the rulebook flat rule (a big hit severs the limb). Listen Up (Detailed Crippling): limb damage is DOUBLED post-armor — 6–12 net cripples it (unusable), 13+ destroys it (needs replacement). W4RST4R: an alternate model where over 8 damage disables the limb and over 12 severs it (either way a Death Save), a head hit over 8 is instantly fatal, and it uses its own hit-location chart (adds the groin). Default: Core.
SETTINGS.HitLocationCoreDisplayHint: When on (default), hits use the standard Core rulebook hit-location chart (head, torso, arms, legs) for every actor. Turn it off to let custom creatures use their own hit-location table instead. (The W4RST4R limb model always uses its own chart regardless of this.)
SETTINGS.VehicleControlEnabledHint: When enabled, vehicles get a 🎲 Control Roll button (sheet header) and the game.cpAugmented.vehicles.controlRoll API. It opens a dialog to roll REF + Driving/Pilot + 1d10 vs a Difficulty Value (Simple 15 / Difficult 20 / Very Difficult 25), and on failure rolls the Control Loss (Core p.112) or Failure (Maximum Metal p.10) table — whichever the active ruleset selects. Works in Core mode without Maximum Metal. Default ON.
SETTINGS.VehicleDamageEnabledHint: When enabled, vehicles get a 💥 Damage button (sheet header) and the game.cpAugmented.vehicles.applyDamage API. Core (p.112) subtracts SP and reduces SDP; Maximum Metal (p.4-6) compares Penetration to Armor Value, rolls the Surface/Minor/Major/Catastrophic damage table, then a hit location with fuel-fire / ammo-cookoff / crew-damage effects (and honors a Damage Control system). The active branch follows the ruleset (Core when Maximum Metal is off). Default ON.
SETTINGS.MmEnabledHint: Master switch for the Maximum Metal military-hardware layer. When OFF (default), vehicles use only the Core 'Vehicles in FNFF' rules (CP2020 p.112) and every MM-only feature is disabled: the Penetration/Armor-Value resolver, composite armor, personnel-vs-anti-vehicle (p.8), area weapons, missiles, the 5-facing vehicle sheet, and the Maximum Metal weapon compendium seeding. Turn ON for the detailed military system. The settings below belong to Maximum Metal.
SETTINGS.VehicleRuleSystemHint: Core = the simple Vehicles-in-FNFF rules (Control Roll vs DV 15/20/25, SP−SDP damage, crash = speed/20 × weight). Maximum Metal = the detailed military system (Penetration vs Armor Value, Surface/Minor/Major/Catastrophic damage, hit-location & crit tables, ACPA). The vehicle sheet shows a single SP in Core mode and all five facings under Maximum Metal.
SETTINGS.VehicleArmorDamageEnabled: Maximum Metal: Armor Damage via Penetration (errata)
SETTINGS.VehicleArmorDamageEnabledHint: Optional errata rule (Maximum Metal p.107). A heavy round (>20mm) erodes the struck facing's SP whether or not it penetrates: SP removed = factor × Penetration (HE ×½, AP/DPU ×0.6, HEAT ×¾, HESH ×1.0). Because Armor Value is derived from SP (SP÷20), sustained fire grinds armor down over time — addressing 'hard to knock down' heavy armor. Applies only under the Maximum Metal resolver. Default OFF; intended for >20mm vehicle weapons (the GM enables it deliberately).
SETTINGS.VehicleMoraleEnabledHint: Optional rule. After a vehicle takes a Minor-or-worse penetrating hit, its crew must pass a morale check — Leadership + 1d10 vs 15 — or bail out / disengage. The damage card shows the 1d10 result and the Leadership needed to hold; the GM adjudicates the consequence. Applies only under the Maximum Metal resolver. Default OFF.
SETTINGS.VehicleArcEnforcementHint: How a weapon mount's firing arc (turret 360° / front / side / rear) is enforced when the target lies outside it. Token facing defines 'front' — rotate a vehicle with Ctrl+scroll (Foundry's 0° points north). Free (default): the Fire dialog only WARNS that the target is outside the mount's arc; you can still fire (GM discretion). Strict: an out-of-arc shot is blocked until the mount can bear — rotate the firing vehicle to face the target, or use a turret. Applies to all vehicle/ACPA weapon mounts, missiles included.
SETTINGS.AmmoBlackhandsPricingHint: Optional Blackhand's Guide ammo pricing, in place of the Core rulebook. Box prices: uniform box-of-100 sizes instead of Core's per-class boxes. Brass ×3: brass-cased loads cost ×3 the base price instead of Core's ×2. Choose either, both, or off (Core). Default: Off.
SETTINGS.VehicleArcEnforcementChoiceStrict: Strict (block out-of-arc shots — keep mounts within bounds)
